code.title: Resume defense drill — 1 bullet
code.detail: Pick one resume bullet. Fill: what I did / tools / problem / result / interview proof. If you cannot, rewrite the claim safer.

code.title: Write 40% SQL story details
code.detail: Which query, which index, how you found the deadlock, what tool, before/after numbers.

code.title: Pin a GitHub repo + README
code.detail: Add a real README to your Employee API or AI report repo.

learn.title: Fix resume/portfolio mismatch
learn.detail: Align dates and version claims across resume, portfolio, LinkedIn.

learn.title: Positioning statement
learn.detail: Refine your 'full-stack .NET, backend-strong, AI-curious' one-liner.

speak.title: Tell me about yourself
speak.detail: Record 75 seconds. Lead with strengths, end with what you want next.

speak.title: Why are you looking for change?
speak.detail: Record 60 seconds. Pull toward product engineering, not away from the bench.

speak.title: Explain one project (STAR)
speak.detail: Pick HRMS / Healthcare / Postal / Wellness. Situation, task, action, result.

speak.title: Explain today's technical topic
speak.detail: Definition, example, tradeoff, resume link. Under 90 seconds.

speak.title: What if you do not know an answer?
speak.detail: Practice the honest pattern: 'I have not done that directly, but my understanding is...'

speak.title: Performance improvement STAR
speak.detail: Tell the healthcare SQL deadlock + 40% improvement story. Hit: situation, your specific task, exact actions (which query, which index, what tool), measurable result.

speak.title: Learning under pressure STAR
speak.detail: Pick a time you had to learn Azure OpenAI or Claude API fast for a client. Walk through: deadline, how you self-taught, what you shipped.

speak.title: Conflict resolution STAR
speak.detail: Describe a real disagreement with a colleague or a design decision. How did you handle it without burning the relationship?

speak.title: Ownership beyond scope STAR
speak.detail: When did you fix or own something that wasn't technically your job? Why did you do it and what happened?

speak.title: Project pride story
speak.detail: Tell the wellness SaaS admin panel or HRMS story as your proudest build. What was the business impact? What did you personally own?

speak.title: Failure / what went wrong STAR
speak.detail: Pick a production bug or a missed deadline. What happened? What did you do? What would you do differently?

learn.title: STAR framework drill
learn.detail: For each of your 4 resume projects, fill this table: Situation / Task / Action (3 specific steps) / Result (number or outcome). If you can't fill Result, the story isn't ready.

learn.title: Red flag handling scripts
learn.detail: Prepare calm answers for: 'I see you were on bench' / 'Why are resume and portfolio dates mismatched?' / 'What do you mean by AI-powered?' Write and read each one out loud.

code.title: SignalR hub — basic implementation
code.detail: Write a minimal SignalR hub with a client-callable method and a server-push method. Understand OnConnectedAsync, OnDisconnectedAsync.

code.title: SignalR wellness SaaS story
code.detail: Write out exactly how the live dashboard worked: Hub setup, groups or broadcast, what the client received, how often updates pushed. This is a live resume claim.

learn.title: SignalR vs WebSocket vs polling
learn.detail: When does SignalR fall back to long-polling? Why is it better than raw WebSockets for most cases? What are the scaling concerns (sticky sessions, Azure SignalR Service)?

code.title: Repository pattern
code.detail: Implement IEmployeeRepository and EmployeeRepository over EF Core. Understand why it decouples business logic from data access and makes testing easier.

code.title: Factory + Strategy pattern
code.detail: Build IAiReportService with AzureOpenAiReportService and MockAiReportService. Wire it via DI and environment config — this is literally your resume project.

code.title: CQRS basics (read/write split)
code.detail: Separate GetEmployees (query) from CreateEmployee (command). No framework needed — just show the pattern in plain C# classes.

learn.title: Repository vs raw DbContext
learn.detail: When does the extra abstraction pay off? When is it overkill? What does Unit of Work add?

learn.title: Common .NET interview patterns
learn.detail: Explain: Singleton, Factory, Repository, Strategy, Decorator, Observer. One sentence + one .NET example each. These come up constantly.

code.title: Fix an N+1 problem
code.detail: Write a query that causes N+1 loads, then fix it with Include() and ThenInclude(). Explain why AsNoTracking() matters for read-only queries.

code.title: Migration + seed
code.detail: Create a migration, apply it, seed 5 employees via HasData. Then rollback and reapply. Get comfortable with the migration flow.

learn.title: EF Core interview questions
learn.detail: Be ready for: Tracking vs no-tracking / lazy vs eager loading / When to use raw SQL / DbContext lifetime / How migrations work. Write one answer for each.

learn.title: Weekly review
learn.detail: What did I complete? What took extra energy? What would make next week smoother? Pick one focus.

learn.title: Rewrite one solution
learn.detail: Redo one thing that felt unfinished this week, from memory.

speak.title: Re-record one answer
speak.detail: Pick one answer from this week and record a cleaner, calmer version.

code.detail: One DSA easy/medium in 25 min. Narrate as you go. Then write what tripped you.

code.title: SQL mock
code.detail: 3 queries under time pressure, explain each plan.

code.title: .NET rapid-fire
code.detail: Answer 10 core .NET questions out loud without notes.

learn.title: Log mock feedback
learn.detail: Top 3 topics to polish from today's mock. Schedule the next pass within 48h.

speak.title: Behavioral mock
speak.detail: Conflict, learning moment, proudest project. STAR, natural delivery.

speak.title: Project defense mock
speak.detail: Defend your hardest resume claim against follow-ups.
